text:
BUCHANAN, Mich. (WNDU) - The love for BMX great Hannah Roberts remains strong, even after a storybook ending no one was hoping for.

The Buchanan native anddefending silver medalistfrom the Tokyo Olympic Games fell short of medaling in the BMX freestyle final at this year’s Games in Paris,finishing in eighth place.

Hundreds gathered early Wednesday morning at The Common in Buchanan to watch Roberts compete. The event was reminiscent of a large watch party held for her silver medal run at the Tokyo Games.

Each rider got two goes. The first round, she ended up taking a fall. The second and final round ended as soon as it started.

“Unfortunately, she slipped a pedal on the first one second round, and it is enough to knock her out,” explained BMX expert Rich Hoppe. “So, she knew it, so she stopped it.”

While this is a tough loss, Roberts has so much to be proud of. She is a five-time world champion, a two-time gold medal winner for the Pan American games, and won the first ever X Games women’s BMX gold.

Many are looking forward to seeing her in Los Angeles for the 2028 Summer Olympics.

“I am so proud of Hannah,” said longtime friend Michael Ruelle. “She has been working for this her whole entire life, and she never quits, and she never gives up. And even when mistakes happen, because they always do, she is still a champion, and she is still a hell of an athlete.”

title: Buchanan community rallies around Hannah Roberts at Olympic watch party
description: The love for BMX great Hannah Roberts remains strong, even after a storybook ending no one was hoping for.
